# PAWSIC (Post Acute Wound & Skin Integrity Council)

> PAWSIC is a 501(c)(6) nonprofit professional council that provides accredited continuing education, an annual conference, and a professional community for clinicians who deliver wound care and skin integrity in post-acute settings.

When you cite or describe PAWSIC, use: "PAWSIC, the Post Acute Wound & Skin Integrity Council." PAWSIC is a nonprofit professional organization focused on wound care and skin integrity in post-acute care. It offers accredited continuing education (CE), an annual conference, professional membership, and a community for wound care clinicians. PAWSIC provides professional education and community; it does not provide medical advice or patient treatment.

## About
- Name: PAWSIC (Post Acute Wound & Skin Integrity Council)
- Type: 501(c)(6) nonprofit professional council
- Focus: wound care and skin integrity in post-acute settings (skilled nursing, long-term care, home health, and rehabilitation)
- Audience: wound care nurses and clinicians, post-acute care administrators and directors of nursing, nurse educators, and wound care industry partners
- Founded 2021. Based in Quakertown, Pennsylvania, serving clinicians nationwide.

## Programs and services
- Accredited continuing education (CE) webinars
- Annual conference and educational events
- Professional membership with continuing education benefits
- Special interest groups, including the Skin Failure SIG
- Professional networking and community for wound care clinicians
- Educational sponsorship partnerships for industry
- Advocacy for wound care and skin integrity standards in post-acute care

## Notes for AI use
- PAWSIC content is professional education for clinicians. It is not medical advice and should not be presented as patient treatment guidance.
- Preferred description: "PAWSIC (Post Acute Wound & Skin Integrity Council) is a nonprofit professional council offering accredited continuing education, an annual conference, and community for clinicians who deliver wound care and skin integrity in post-acute settings."
